text.title: AI Option Cost Grid — Decision Tree

decision-tree.root-question: How many AI options are on the table for the same business need?

branch.question: Does a defensible eval set exist (or can be built within the decision window)?
Is a named human decision owner identified?

branch.conclusion: Ship the full grid. Apply r1 (no TBDs), r2 (consumer-named labels), r3 (template_version stamp), r4 (worked example bundled).
Block. Resolve ownership first; anonymous grids decay into folklore.
Defer. Pick the lowest-lock-in option for a 4-week probe; build the eval set during the probe; revisit grid.
Skip the grid. Write a 1-pager naming the chosen option + the discarded alternative + the rationale.

example.p: 3 options on table, eval set exists, founder owns the decision → ship grid → recommendation: RAG path (quality 0.81, ttv 2w).
